content:
💡 plain-app | Kotlin
🎯 Primary Use Case: Securely managing and accessing an Android phone's data and features from a web browser on the same local network, without relying on cloud services.
✨ Key Features: • Web-based phone management dashboard • Local-only operation (no cloud) • End-to-end encryption (TLS + XChaCha20-Poly1305) • File browsing, upload, download • Media viewing and streaming
📖 Summary: PlainApp is an open-source Android application that provides a self-hosted, web-based interface for managing your phone's files, media, contacts, SMS, and more from any device on the same network. It prioritizes privacy and security with local-only operation and end-to-end encryption.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 deb-get | Shell
🎯 Primary Use Case: Managing .deb packages from third-party sources that are not available in official repositories, simplifying software installation and updates on Debian-based systems.
✨ Key Features: • Install .deb packages from GitHub releases or direct download URLs • Update and upgrade packages installed via deb-get • Search for available applications • Remove and purge packages • Support for GitHub Personal Access Tokens for API authorization
📖 Summary: deb-get is a command-line tool that extends apt-get functionality to manage .deb packages published via GitHub releases or direct downloads. It simplifies the installation, upgrade, and removal of software not available in standard Ubuntu/Debian repositories.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 netwatch | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: Real-time network monitoring and troubleshooting directly within the terminal, providing both a broad overview and detailed packet-level insights.
✨ Key Features: • Real-time network diagnostics • Terminal-based interface (TUI) • Interface statistics with sparklines • Connection monitoring with process names and PIDs • Gateway and DNS health checks with latency heatmap
📖 Summary: NetWatch is a real-time network diagnostics tool for the terminal, offering instant visibility into network interfaces, connections, and packet data. It provides both high-level overviews and deep packet inspection capabilities, along with a flight recorder feature to capture transient network events.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ agents | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Building and deploying AI agents to automate trading on Polymarket prediction markets.
✨ Key Features: • Integration with Polymarket API • AI agent utilities for prediction markets • Local and remote RAG support • Data sourcing from betting services, news providers, and web search • Comprehensive LLM tools for prompt engineering
📖 Summary: Polymarket Agents is a Python-based developer framework for building AI agents that can autonomously trade on the Polymarket prediction market platform. It provides tools for integrating with the Polymarket API, sourcing data, and utilizing LLMs for market predictions and trade execution.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
